text.title: Twitter/X Budget, Bidding, and Measurement
text.summary: Cost benchmarks, bidding strategy selection, and key metric targets for Twitter/X campaigns.

list.item: CPM: $5-15 (lower than Meta/LinkedIn for broad audiences)

rule.statement: Start with a minimum of $20/day; use $50+/day for meaningful testing data within 7 days.
rule.rationale: Below $20/day, impressions are too sparse to generate statistically valid A/B comparison data between ad variations in a reasonable timeframe.

list.item: Automatic bid — recommended for new campaigns; lets Twitter find efficient delivery
Maximum bid — set per-click ceiling; use when CPCs exceed acceptable threshold
Target bid — optimize toward a specific average cost; use after 2 weeks of baseline data
Cost per engagement: under $0.50, under $0.25 is excellent

rule.statement: Refresh creative every 2 weeks — Twitter audiences are more homogeneous than Meta's, causing ad fatigue faster due to high impression repetition within small niche audiences.
rule.rationale: Niche follower-lookalike audiences on Twitter can be as small as 50-200K users; at $50/day, frequency reaches 3+ within 10-14 days, after which engagement rates drop sharply.

list.item: Install Twitter pixel on all pages
Add UTM parameters to all destination URLs
Monitor replies — Twitter ads generate comments that require active moderation
